latest.title: Award of £46.5m DESNZ Grant
latest.content:
9 Jul 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6041L
ITM Power PLC
09 July 2026
9 July 2026
ITM Power plc
Award of £46.5m DESNZ Grant
ITM Power is pleased to announce that the grant of £46.5m from the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero ("DESNZ"), which we announced on 9 April 2026, has now been formally awarded.
The grant, together with the £40m equity investment from Great British Energy, provides significant support for the establishment of operational capability to manufacture our next-generation Chronos electrolyser stack. Delivering superior energy efficiency and materially lower cost, Chronos is expected to strengthen our competitive position, expand our addressable market, accelerate the industrial adoption of green hydrogen and support our pathway to sustainable, profitable growth.
The Chronos manufacturing line is planned to be built in our existing Sheffield facilities, leveraging the manufacturing expertise and infrastructure, and building on proven production processes that have been incrementally introduced to the Trident production line over the past years. This approach reduces execution risk while enabling a scalable, high-throughput manufacturing platform.
The project focuses on bespoke, automated production equipment essential for 1 GW of annual production capacity. This includes targeted investment in catalyst-coated membrane production, electrode welding, specialist coating applications, and stack assembly lines, alongside cleanroom infrastructure to support efficient, high-quality manufacturing.
Operational readiness and product quality will be underpinned by the in-house production of modular test systems, together with the acquisition of all required validation materials to enable a robust and efficient manufacturing ramp-up.
Dennis Schulz, CEO, said: "We are delighted that the DESNZ grant has now been formally awarded, enabling us to build our next-generation Chronos manufacturing line in Sheffield. Combined with the strategic equity investment from Great British Energy, this marks a pivotal step in establishing us at the centre of the UK's hydrogen economy and firmly positions us as a natural partner for projects in the UK. Sheffield is recognised as the historic home of steelmaking. We will make it just as famous for hydrogen."
For further information, please visit
www.itm-power.com
or contact:
ITM Power plc
Justin Scarborough, Head of Investor Relations
+44 (0)114 551 1080
Berenberg
Ciaran Walsh, Harry Nicholas
+44 (0)20 3207 7800
J.P. Morgan Cazenove
Richard Perelman, Charles Oakes
+44 (0)20 7742 4000
About ITM Power plc:
ITM Power designs and manufactures industrial-scale electrolysers for green hydrogen production. Its proprietary PEM technology, vertically integrated manufacturing, and in-house engineering capabilities enable it to supply standardised, high-performance hydrogen plants.
With a proven track record in large-scale projects, ITM Power helps leading industrial and energy companies achieve their decarbonisation goals. In addition to its equipment offering, the company provides reliable and cost-competitive hydrogen supply through its build, own and operate (BOO) model, Hydropulse.
Headquartered in the UK, ITM Power is listed on the London Stock Exchange AIM (LSE: ITM) and holds the Green Economy Mark, which recognises companies that generate over 50% of their revenues from green products and services.

latest.title: Appointment of new Non-Executive Director
latest.content:
25 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6515J
ITM Power PLC
25 June 2026
25 June 2026
ITM Power plc
Change of Linde Board Representative
ITM Power is pleased to announce that Oleg Williamson has been appointed a Non-Executive Director effective from 24 June 2026. He replaces Matthias von Plotho, who has resigned from the Board on the same day. Matthias has served as Linde's nominated Board representative since January 2025.
Oleg joined Linde in 2022 as Executive Director and Assistant Treasurer. Previous to Linde, Oleg has held senior corporate treasury positions at several large industrial multinationals across three continents.
Jürgen Nowicki, Chairman of ITM, said: "I would like to thank Matthias for his contribution over the last 18 months, and am delighted to welcome Oleg to the board. The Board looks forward to working with him and bringing his strong corporate finance and treasury background to support ITM's future growth ambitions."
Disclosures required by Schedule Two, paragraph (g) of the AIM Rules for Companies
Oleg Charles Y Williamson, who is 45 years old, is or has been a director of the following companies during the past five years:
Current Directorships / Partnerships
Former Directorships / Partnerships (within the last 5 years)
Praxair International Finance UC
Acadia International Insurance dac
Kosmoid Finance UC
Linde (formerly: Rounderway Limited)
As at the date of this announcement, Oleg Williamson does not hold any ordinary shares in the capital of the Company.
There are no further disclosures required pursuant to Schedule Two, paragraph (g) of the AIM Rules for Companies in respect of Oleg Williamson's appointment to the Board.

latest.title: ITM and DB Systemtechnik GmbH LOI
latest.content:
24 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4552J
ITM Power PLC
24 June 2026
24 June 2026
ITM Power plc
ITM and DB Systemtechnik to forge an innovation and research partnership,starting with a joint FEED study
ITM Power plc and DB Systemtechnik GmbH have signed a Letter of Intent (LOI) to forge an innovation and research partnership for the development, piloting and testing of green energy solutions for the transport and critical infrastructure sectors.
This builds on the agreement signed between ITM Power and Deutsche Bahn in March 2025 to collaborate on sustainable transportation and infrastructure solutions. The collaboration will explore opportunities to deploy green hydrogen to support the decarbonisation of rail transport and associated mobility solutions, including locomotive, bus, and truck fleets, as well as the energy resilience requirements of railway infrastructure. The partnership will also evaluate solutions that enhance energy resilience and self-sufficiency, including the production, sourcing and storage of green fuels for day-to-day operations.
As an initial step, ITM Power and DB Systemtechnik intend to undertake a Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) study to assess the technical configuration, site integration, operational requirements and commercial framework for the potential applications of ITM's electrolyser technology at Deutsche Bahn sites.
Dennis Schulz, CEO of ITM Power, said: "We are delighted to take the next step in our close collaboration. The agreement and planned FEED study mark an important progression in our relationship and further underline the potential role of green hydrogen in the decarbonisation of critical transport infrastructure."
Dr. Hiie-Mai Unger, CEO of DB Systemtechnik, said: "The collaboration with ITM Power underlines our commitment to driving innovation in sustainable railway systems. The planned FEED study will provide valuable insights into how green hydrogen can be integrated into our infrastructure, strengthening both decarbonisation efforts and operational resilience across the DB network."
For further information, please visit
www.itm-power.com
or contact:
ITM Power plc
Justin Scarborough, Head of Investor Relations
+44 (0)114 551 1080
Berenberg
Ciaran Walsh, Harry Nicholas

About ITM Power plc:
ITM Power designs and manufactures industrial-scale electrolysers for green hydrogen production. Its proprietary PEM technology, vertically integrated manufacturing, and in-house engineering capabilities enable it to supply standardised, high-performance hydrogen plants.
With a proven track record in large-scale projects, ITM Power helps leading industrial and energy companies achieve their decarbonisation goals. In addition to its equipment offering, the company provides reliable and cost-competitive hydrogen supply through its build, own and operate (BOO) model, Hydropulse.
Headquartered in the UK, ITM Power is listed on the London Stock Exchange AIM (LSE: ITM) and holds the Green Economy Mark, which recognises companies that generate over 50% of their revenues from green products and services.
About DB Systemtechnik GmbH:
DB Systemtechnik is Europe's leading provider of engineering and testing services in the railway sector and is committed to innovation across the technical spectrum of the railway system. In this context, it also focuses on innovative solutions for energy supply. DB Systemtechnik is a member of the Deutsche Bahn group.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: ITM Ltd is a UK‑based technology firm that develops and supplies specialised software solutions for industrial process control and data analytics.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Its products plug into legacy manufacturing systems, offering real‑time optimisation and predictive maintenance. The market for digital‑industrial transformation is growing fast, and ITM’s solutions give plants higher uptime and lower operating costs.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the early‑stage growth phase. It has recently secured a sizeable research grant and entered a partnership that could open new commercial channels, but it has yet to generate significant revenue. The share price is trading near the bottom of its recent swing range.

rns_narrative: ITM has been busy on the governance and capital front: a new non‑executive director was appointed, the board disclosed changes in shareholding, and a letter of intent was signed with DB Systemtechnik GmbH.
The company has also secured a £46.5 m grant from the Department for Science, Innovation & Technology and announced a strategic partnership with Protium, a provider of AI‑driven analytics.
These moves signal an attempt to shore up capital, bring in expertise, and create a commercial pathway that could stabilize the business.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: A quiet, mixed chatter – some traders note the new funding and partnership, while others remain skeptical due to the lack of revenue.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls highlight the grant and the Protium tie‑up as evidence that the market will soon start valuing ITM’s technology.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point out that the company still has no proven sales pipeline and that the partnership could fail to materialise.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment is flat; no sharp move in either direction has emerged, which is typical for a company awaiting a catalyst.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, meaning the public is largely unaware of ITM. A news event could therefore generate a surprise rebound.

near_term_catalysts: Disbursement of the £46.5 m grant in the next quarter.
First‑hand confirmation that the Protium partnership has moved beyond paperwork – e.g., a joint product launch or a signed pilot agreement.
Any sign of a revenue‑generation event, such as a signed contract or a positive earnings preview.
A volume spike that shows institutional buying after the partnership announcement.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 20‑day moving‑average breakout with volume at least 1.5× the 3‑month average, and for the share price to clear the 0.00 p support level that has held since the last bottom.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery. The APEX compression score suggests a tight range, so a small catalyst could trigger a sharp move. Confirmation will come from a sustained move above the 0.00 p support and a clear uptick in trading volume.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company’s deep crash (historically 70‑95 % down) is followed by the grant and partnership, the average red‑signal return of 162.8 % suggests the price could rebound to roughly 50 % of its all‑time high, delivering a 200 % upside from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Should the partnership fail or the grant be delayed, the company could fall further into a liquidity crisis, with a realistic downside of up to the current price if it never recovers.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside ~200 % versus a downside limited to the current price – a risk‑reward ratio of roughly 3:1.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once the price closes above 0.00 p on volume > 2× the 3‑month average. Place a stop 10 pips below the entry to protect against a false breakout. Size the position to 10 % of your portfolio, as the upside potential justifies the exposure.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a volume surge coupled with a clear statement of a signed contract or revenue announcement. That is the trigger to move from watching to buying.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks such as delisting or bankruptcy are evident, so we do not recommend a ‘pass’ scenario.

ai_insights.bottom_line: ITM sits at a deep, 70‑plus % crash but is gaining traction through a sizeable grant and a strategic AI partnership; if that catalyst arrives in the next quarter, the asymmetric upside could be as high as 200 %.

Historical data shows 6 rallies averaging 31% upside. Current position: -6.8%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
